status: Passed (CLI self-test + unit); VM fsck_hfs + kernel mount still required before matrix flip
capability: When a special-file B-tree split exhausts the node pool, the writer now grows the special-file fork (allocation bitmap + fork extents + node map + total/free node counts) instead of failing closed. A generic engine (planSpecialTreeNodePoolGrowth / applySpecialTreeNodePoolGrowth / ensureSpecialTreeFreeNodes over an HfsSpecialTreeTarget) drives all three special-file B-trees: catalog, extents-overflow, and attributes. Node 0 of every B-tree special file lives at fork offset 0, so reads/writes go through readForkBytes/writeForkBytes. Reactive: a mutation is tried first and only grows+retries on a genuine node-exhaustion failure, so non-exhausting mutations are unchanged.

trees_wired.catalog: ensureCatalogFreeNodes via withCatalogNodePoolGrowth around prepareEmptyFileCreate / prepareFileCreate / prepareEmptyFolderCreate / prepareCatalogRenameMove / prepareCatalogTreeMutation.
trees_wired.extents_overflow: ensureExtentsFreeNodes via grow-retry around prepareExtentsTreeMutation. Note: with 1024-byte extents nodes in 4096-byte blocks (4 nodes/block minimum) and root-leaf-only splits, extents-tree exhaustion is effectively unreachable today; the wiring is defensive for the future deeper-tree engine (H-b).
trees_wired.attributes: ensureAttributesFreeNodes via grow-retry around prepareAttributesTreeInsert; the attributes header is parsed on demand (loadAttributeHeader re-reads the grown header after growth).

code: include/sak/partition_hfs_internal.h: HfsSpecialTreeTarget + planSpecialTreeNodePoolGrowth / applySpecialTreeNodePoolGrowth / ensureSpecialTreeFreeNodes / ensureCatalogFreeNodes / ensureExtentsFreeNodes / ensureAttributesFreeNodes / writeSpecialFileForkToVolumeHeader / blockersIndicateNodeExhaustion / withCatalogNodePoolGrowth + grow-retry wrappers on prepareExtentsTreeMutation and prepareAttributesTreeInsert.

Bounds (fail-closed, not yet supported; H-b/H-d): growth that would span multiple B-tree map nodes (~63k nodes at 8 KiB = ~0.5 GiB single tree), or that would push the special-file fork past 8 volume-header extents (extents-overflow of the special-file fork itself).
unit_test: tests/unit/test_partition_manager_core.cpp::hfsFileSystemWriter_growsCatalogNodePoolOrFailsClosed - exhausts a free_nodes=1 catalog, asserts growth succeeds (total_nodes up, free_blocks down, file created, S.A.K. consistency clean), and asserts fail-closed when no free blocks remain. This exercises the complete generic engine that the extents + attributes trees reuse.
test_coverage_note: Catalog growth: direct unit test + bundled fsck (below). Attributes-tree growth: direct unit test hfsFileSystemWriter_growsAttributesNodePoolOnRootLeafSplit (fills the single leaf until a create forces a root-leaf split under free_nodes=0; asserts the node pool grew, free_blocks dropped, and a created attribute reads back) - proves the generic engine on a second tree geometry. Extents-tree growth: wired through the same engine but exhaustion is unreachable in the current single-leaf-split geometry (1024-byte nodes in 4096-byte blocks = 4-node minimum); the wiring is defensive for the future deeper-tree engine (H-b).

bundled_fsck_self_test.method: newfs_hfs an 8 MB HFS+ volume (GROWTEST); sak_hfs_writer_cli create-empty-files-image --file-count 800 to exhaust+grow the catalog; fsck_hfs -n -f.
bundled_fsck_self_test.fsck_result: The volume GROWTEST appears to be OK.

gates: clang-format + lizard (CCN<=10) + cppcheck clean; full build + 139/140 ctest (only the pre-existing deferred test_file_explorer_types).
follow_ups: External gate before flipping the H-a matrix cell to Yes: Apple fsck_hfs + kernel RW mount in the macOS VM, plus physical-USB destructive + crash + rollback (per APFS_HFS_FULL_DRIVER_WRITE_PLAN.md milestone H1).
Extend node-pool growth past the single-map-node and 8-extent (extents-overflow) bounds.
Apply the same growth to the attributes and extents-overflow B-tree special files.
